verified_title: Secretion of cyclolysin, the calmodulin-sensitive adenylate cyclase-haemolysin bifunctional protein of Bordetella pertussis.
note: Glaser 1988 EMBO J; cyaA bifunctional adenylate cyclase-haemolysin secreted via cyaBDE (HlyBD-like) T1SS. Matches hint DOI exactly.

verified_title: Legionella pneumophila secretes an endoglucanase that belongs to the family-5 of glycosyl hydrolases and is dependent upon type II secretion.
note: Pearce & Cianciotto 2009 FEMS Microbiol Lett: celA mutant abolishes secreted endoglucanase, lspF/T2S-dependent. Stronger CelA-specific primary than hint's DebRoy 2006 PNAS (10.1073/pnas.0608279103, T2 secretome survey). DebRoy is valid too but not CelA-centred.

verified_title: The Salmonella enterica serovar typhimurium-encoded type III secretion systems can translocate Chlamydia trachomatis proteins into the cytosol of host cells.
note: Ho & Starnbach 2005 Infect Immun directly shows C. trachomatis CopN is T3SS-translocated into host cytosol. Hint's Archuleta 2011 (10.1074/jbc.M111.258426, Crossref-confirmed 'CopN sequesters tubulin') is CopN functional characterization but does NOT demonstrate secretion. Old DOI M610343200 is dead. Proposing the translocation paper.

verified_title: The Citrobacter rodentium genome sequence reveals convergent evolution with human pathogenic Escherichia coli.
note: Petty 2009/2010 J Bacteriol: C. rodentium ICC168 genome; documents LEE T3SS + core effector repertoire (EspA among the 22 core EPEC effectors). Genome/repertoire paper, not a per-effector translocation assay, but the answer-key organism anchor. Matches hint DOI.

verified_title: The Citrobacter rodentium genome sequence reveals convergent evolution with human pathogenic Escherichia coli.
note: Petty 2009/2010 J Bacteriol; C. rodentium ICC168 LEE effector repertoire incl EspZ. Genome/repertoire anchor. Matches hint.

verified_title: Proteomic and functional analysis of the suite of Ysp proteins exported by the Ysa type III secretion system of Yersinia enterocolitica Biovar 1B.
note: Matsumoto & Young 2006 Mol Microbiol: YspE-CyaA fusion translocated by Ysa T3SS (Ysa-dependent cAMP increase). Old DOI JB.01816-08 dead. Matches hint.

verified_title: The Citrobacter rodentium genome sequence reveals convergent evolution with human pathogenic Escherichia coli.
note: Duplicate of R082 (same gene/organism). Petty genome/LEE repertoire anchor. Matches hint.

note: Petty 2009/2010; C. rodentium ICC168 LEE effector EspH. Genome/repertoire anchor. Matches hint.

verified_title: The Citrobacter rodentium genome sequence reveals convergent evolution with human pathogenic Escherichia coli.
note: Duplicate of R084. Petty genome/LEE anchor. Matches hint.

verified_title: The Citrobacter rodentium genome sequence reveals convergent evolution with human pathogenic Escherichia coli.
note: Petty 2009/2010; C. rodentium ICC168 LEE effector Map. Genome/repertoire anchor. Matches hint.

verified_title: Recognition of the Agrobacterium tumefaciens VirE2 translocation signal by the VirB/D4 transport system does not require VirE1.
note: Vergunst 2003 Plant Physiol: CRAFT/Cre assay shows VirE3 C-terminal signal mediates VirB/D4 translocation into host cells. HINT DOI 10.1073/pnas.0500396102 IS INVALID (404 Crossref+EuropePMC). Real Vergunst 2005 PNAS is 10.1073/pnas.0406241102 (PMID 15644442) but that one characterizes VirF signal, not VirE3. This 2003 paper is the correct VirE3-translocation primary.

verified_title: A bipartite signal mediates the transfer of type IV secretion substrates of Bartonella henselae into human cells.
note: Schulein 2005 PNAS: identifies Beps A-G (incl BepD) as VirB/VirD4-translocated effectors into human endothelial cells. Matches hint; correctly replaces Okujava 2014 (BepE-centred).

verified_title: Diverse type VI secretion phospholipases are functionally plastic antibacterial effectors.
note: Russell 2013 Nature (PMID 23552891): Tle phospholipase superfamily; PldA is founding H2-T6SS substrate in P. aeruginosa, antibacterial. Old DOI nature11600 = protein-design paper. Matches hint. (NB ambiguous Nature 2013 vol496: 23552910 is unrelated commentary; correct=23552891.)

verified_title: Diverse type VI secretion phospholipases are functionally plastic antibacterial effectors.
note: Russell 2013 Nature; Tle phospholipase superfamily incl Tle4 family, H2-T6SS P. aeruginosa. Matches hint.

verified_title: The Pseudomonas aeruginosa Type VI Secretion PGAP1-like Effector Induces Host Autophagy by Activating Endoplasmic Reticulum Stress.
note: Jiang 2016 Cell Rep (PMID 27477276): TplE H2-T6SS PGAP1-like phospholipase, periplasmic delivery to rival bacteria + host ER. Old DOI celrep.2016.07.020 = lung-regeneration paper. Matches hint.

verified_title: Diverse type VI secretion phospholipases are functionally plastic antibacterial effectors.
note: Russell 2013 Nature: Tle lipase superfamily covers B. thailandensis Tle1-type phospholipases (T6SS antibacterial). Old DOI chom.2012.04.007 is the Tae AMIDASE paper (wrong enzyme class). Matches hint. Lipase-superfamily primary, not a B.thailandensis-strain-specific assay.

verified_title: Type VI Secretion System Transports Zn2+ to Combat Multiple Stresses and Host Immunity.
note: Wang 2015 PLoS Pathog: YezP (YPK_3549) is a T6SS-4-translocated Zn-binding substrate in Y. pseudotuberculosis. Old DOI cr.2013.150 = H7N9 commentary. Matches hint.

verified_title: A widespread bacterial type VI secretion effector superfamily identified using a heuristic approach.
note: Russell 2012 Cell Host Microbe: Tae peptidoglycan-amidase T6SS effector superfamily (4 families incl Salmonella Tae4). Old DOI chom.2012.06.002 = Drosophila PGRP paper. Matches hint.

verified_title: A phospholipase A1 antibacterial Type VI secretion effector interacts directly with the C-terminal domain of the VgrG spike protein for delivery.
note: Flaugnatti 2016 Mol Microbiol: E. coli (entero-aggregative) Sci-1 T6SS Tle1 phospholipase A1, VgrG1-delivered into prey periplasm. Old DOI fcimb.2018.00074 = HSV-1 paper. Matches hint.
